free_text: Fission product data measured at Los Alamos for
fission spectrum and thermal neutrons on 239Pu, 235U,
BIG TEN critical assembly.  The peak of
the neutron energy spectrum was approximately 300 keV,
the average energy was approximately 600 keV. External
ion chambers and 235U-sealed fission chambers external
to the assembly were used to provide highly redundant
power-flux level time history monitoring during all
The irradiations were conducted in the thermal
column of OWR, that corresponded to a Cd ratio of
5,000-10,000, or a fully thermalized, isotropic source.
Fission count data associated with the NIST fission
chamber was translated to absolute fission rates by
application of a number of corrections (dead-time loss,
fission fragment loss, fissions in minor isotopes,
scattering and absorption). Cumulative corrections due
to these factors to the absolute fission rate ranged

The 239Pu foils were rolled, punched, weighed, and cold
pressure welded into capsules of 0.177-mm thick
aluminum foil;all prepared in an Ar-filled glove box.
Reference foils 235U3O8 and 239PuO2 were prepared by
vapor deposition onto polished platinum disks. The
material was deposited using electron bombardment

The 235U foils were prepared by rolling to a nominal
thickness of 0.13 mm. Disks (12.7 mm-diameter) were
stamped from the rolled stock in air. The disk was then
etched in nitric acid to remove oxide coating, rinsed
in alcohol, and placed in a argon-filled glovebox,
where they were weighed (foil mass 277.972 mg +-0.5%.
